description: Add missing RetroUI design system primitives (Menu, Badge, Progress) to the side panel retro component library, adapted for the project's theme system.

userStories.title: Add RetroUI Menu primitive
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a themed Menu (dropdown menu) retro primitive so that dropdown menus across the side panel use a consistent RetroUI style instead of hand-styled Radix primitives.
acceptanceCriteria: Menu wraps @radix-ui/react-dropdown-menu with RetroUI styling adapted for the theme (border-border instead of hardcoded colors, bg-card for content, theme-aware hover states)
Menu is exported as a compound component with sub-components: Menu.Trigger, Menu.Content, Menu.Item (matching the Object.assign pattern used by Accordion, Alert, etc.)
Menu.Content has border-2 border-border bg-card rounded shadow-md styling
Menu.Item has hover:bg-accent data-[highlighted]:bg-accent cursor-pointer styling
A Storybook story file Menu.stories.tsx exists alongside the component with at minimum: default, multiple items, and disabled item states
userStories.notes: Reference implementation: ~/Downloads/RetroUI-main/components/retroui/Menu.tsx. Key adaptations needed: replace bg-white with bg-card, replace text-black with text-foreground, replace hardcoded border colors with border-border, replace hover:bg-primary with hover:bg-accent (consistent with existing dropdown styling in platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/ConfirmationDialog.tsx:73). Follow the exact same patterns as existing retro components — use cn() from platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/lib/cn.ts, use Object.assign compound pattern. Do NOT add 'use client' directive (this is not Next.js). All retro components live in platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/retro/.

userStories.title: Add RetroUI Badge primitive
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a themed Badge retro primitive so that status indicators and labels use a consistent RetroUI style.
acceptanceCriteria: Badge uses cva with variant options: default, outline, solid, surface
Badge uses cva with size options: sm, md, lg
Badge is theme-aware: uses bg-muted, text-muted-foreground, bg-foreground, text-background, bg-primary, text-primary-foreground (no hardcoded colors)
Badge uses cn() for className composition
A Storybook story file Badge.stories.tsx exists alongside the component covering all variants and sizes
userStories.notes: Reference implementation: ~/Downloads/RetroUI-main/components/retroui/Badge.tsx. The reference is already mostly theme-aware. Key adaptation: replace outline-2 outline-foreground with border-2 border-foreground (the side panel uses border-2 pattern consistently, not outline). Follow the same export pattern as other retro components (named export, not default). File location: platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/retro/Badge.tsx.

userStories.title: Add RetroUI Progress primitive
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a themed Progress bar retro primitive so that progress indicators use a consistent RetroUI style instead of hand-built divs.
acceptanceCriteria: Progress root has border-2 border-border bg-background styling; indicator has bg-primary
Progress uses cn() for className composition and accepts className prop
Progress is exported with forwardRef for ref forwarding
A Storybook story file Progress.stories.tsx exists alongside the component with states: 0%, 50%, 100%, and a custom className variant
@radix-ui/react-progress is added to platform/browser-extension/package.json if not already present
userStories.notes: Reference implementation: ~/Downloads/RetroUI-main/components/retroui/Progress.tsx. Keep it simple — the reference is only 28 lines. Check platform/browser-extension/package.json for @radix-ui/react-progress — if missing, add it with 'bun add @radix-ui/react-progress' from platform/browser-extension/. File location: platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/retro/Progress.tsx. The Progress component will be consumed by ConfirmationDialog's CountdownBar in a subsequent PRD.
